title: Taxi refusal
header: I am writing to complain that I was refused service by {{operator}} (or one of its drivers) on {{date}} at {{location}}.
legalParagraph: Under sections 165 and 166 of the Equality Act 2010, taxi drivers (and private hire dispatchers) must not refuse to carry wheelchair users or assistance dog owners, and must not charge extra. A refusal on those grounds is a criminal offence carrying a fine, and the licensing authority is empowered to take regulatory action.
ask: I would like the driver and journey identified, a full investigation, and confirmation that this incident has been reported to the licensing authority. I would also like a written response within 20 working days setting out the corrective action taken.
